rubric_results.check: Title is non-empty and plausibly the document's title?
rubric_results.note: The title references TARP (Tunnel and Reservoir Plan), a well-known Chicago-area flood control project, and specifies the Lower Des Plaines component with 'environmental impact statement' — highly plausible as an EIS title.

rubric_results.check: Length is reasonable (not a sentence fragment, not the whole abstract)?
rubric_results.note: Title is concise and appropriately scoped for a document title.

rubric_results.check: If two sources (NUL + Haiku) — do they roughly agree?
rubric_results.note: Only one source (NUL) provided; no Haiku source to compare.

title.notes: The extracted title is plausible and well-formed for an EIS document related to the TARP project in the Lower Des Plaines area. However, no cited page text was provided to verify the extraction, and only a single source (NUL) was used. Confidence is medium due to the absence of corroborating page text or a second source.

rubric_results.check: Year falls within 1969 ≤ year ≤ current year?
rubric_results.note: 2026 is a future year beyond the current year (2025), failing the upper bound; regex value 1977 would pass

rubric_results.check: Year present in NUL metadata OR found on first 3 pages?
rubric_results.note: NUL metadata reports 2026 and regex found 1977 on first 3 pages; both sources present but conflicting

rubric_results.check: If NUL and regex disagree, value reflects this with a low-confidence flag?
rubric_results.note: Extraction correctly flagged low confidence and noted the NUL=2026 vs regex=1977 disagreement

year.notes: NUL metadata yields 2026, which is a future year and almost certainly erroneous (possibly a metadata entry error or digitization artifact). The regex value of 1977 falls within the valid EIS range and is far more plausible for an Environmental Impact Statement. No cited page text was provided to adjudicate. A human reviewer should inspect the document directly to confirm the correct year, with 1977 being the likely correct value.

rubric_results.check: Type is one of: Draft, Final, Supplemental, ROD, Unknown?
rubric_results.note: Draft is a valid recognized EIS type.

rubric_results.check: Regex hit on first page and Sonnet on first 2 pages agree?
rubric_results.note: Both sources listed agree on 'Draft'.

rubric_results.check: Evidence phrase supports the chosen type?
rubric_results.note: 'DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT' directly and unambiguously supports the Draft classification.

rubric_results.check: Cited pages provided for independent verification?
rubric_results.note: No cited page text was provided, so the evidence phrase cannot be independently verified against the source document.

eis_type.notes: The extracted value of 'Draft' is strongly supported by the evidence phrase 'DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT,' and both extraction methods agree. However, no cited page text was provided, preventing independent verification of the evidence phrase against the actual document. The extraction is accepted with the caveat that cited pages should be included in future submissions for full auditability.

rubric_results.check: All listed agencies appear in NUL contributors OR on cited title pages?
rubric_results.note: United States. Environmental Protection Agency is sourced from NUL and is a well-recognized agency name; no title page text provided to cross-check, but NUL attribution is accepted.

rubric_results.check: Joint-lead (>2 agencies) flagged for human review?
rubric_results.note: Only one agency listed; joint-lead flag not required.

rubric_results.check: No role text left in agency names?
rubric_results.note: The agency name 'United States. Environmental Protection Agency' contains no role descriptors such as '(issuing body)' or similar.

lead_agency.notes: A single lead agency, United States. Environmental Protection Agency, is extracted and sourced from NUL with no role text present. No cited title pages were provided for secondary verification, but the NUL source and clean formatting support a passing verdict. No joint-lead concerns apply.

rubric_results.check: project_description text supported by cited pages
rubric_results.note: Cited pages confirm EPA construction grant for TARP Phase I tunnels, Lower Des Plaines system with ~26 miles of tunnel, 55 drop shafts, 10 access shafts, 5 construction shafts, 80 collecting structures, 1 pumping station, $346.9M Des Plaines cost, ~$1.46B total Phase I tunnel cost. Depth range stated as 150-325 feet: pages say 150-290 feet for conveyance tunnels generally, but drop shafts are described as 250-foot deep examples; the '325 feet' figure is not directly confirmed in the cited text.

rubric_results.check: Numbers, places, and dates match cited pages
rubric_results.note: The extraction states tunnels are '150-325 feet underground' but the cited text says 'located 150 to 290 feet below ground level.' The 325-foot figure does not appear in the cited pages. All other numbers (860 sq miles, 120 municipalities, 5.5M population, 9.6%/11.2% unemployment, $346.9M, $1.46B, 3.78M cu yd spoil, 84 disabling injuries, 23 plans, 6 eliminated, 51 subplans, 8 parameters, 15 criteria) match the cited text.

rubric_results.check: public_response limited to main doc (based_on_main_doc_only=true)
rubric_results.note: The public_response field is marked based_on_main_doc_only=true and the content references meetings and hearings described within the EIS document itself.

rubric_results.check: No invention or hallucinated detail beyond cited content
rubric_results.note: The '150-325 feet underground' depth range for the Des Plaines tunnel is not supported; cited pages state 150-290 feet. The public_response mentions specific hearing dates (October 1972, July 1973, 1974, July 1975, September 1975, November 1975) and the Cook County Forest Preserve District request for rock stockpiling for bicycle paths/equestrian trails/winter recreation, and NuEarth positive reaction—these details are plausible from the EIS context but the specific hearing dates and Forest Preserve request are not visible in the provided cited page text, raising hallucination risk.

summary.notes: The extraction contains at least one clear numerical discrepancy: the tunnel depth is stated as '150-325 feet' but the cited pages specify '150 to 290 feet below ground level.' Additionally, several specific details in the public_response section (particular hearing dates, the Cook County Forest Preserve District's formal request for rock stockpiling for specific recreational uses, and the NuEarth positive reaction) are not visible in the provided cited page text and may be drawn from pages not included in the citation pull. The extraction should be re-done with verified depth figures and confirmed public_response sourcing.

rubric_results.note: No alternatives were extracted and no cited pages were provided, so this check cannot be performed.

rubric_results.note: No cited text was provided, so it is impossible to verify whether a No Action alternative should have been included.

rubric_results.check: Names and descriptions match terminology used in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: No extracted names or descriptions exist to compare against any cited text.

rubric_results.check: No outside-knowledge alternatives added that aren't in the chapter?
rubric_results.note: The extracted value is an empty list, so no outside-knowledge alternatives were introduced.

alternatives.notes: The extraction returned an empty result with no cited pages, citing a structural detection failure and a deliberate skip per the v2 plan. Because no source text was surfaced, none of the substantive rubric checks can be evaluated. A human reviewer should locate the Alternatives chapter in the EIS and either re-run extraction with corrected logic or manually populate the alternatives field.

rubric_results.check: Each chosen theme is supported by the doc summary fields?
rubric_results.note: No cited page text was provided, so direct support from the document cannot be verified. The justification is plausible but unverifiable without source text.

rubric_results.check: Theme names come from the fixed taxonomy verbatim?
rubric_results.note: Cannot confirm against taxonomy without access to the fixed taxonomy list; names appear plausible but 'Industrial Production and Materials' cannot be verified as verbatim.

rubric_results.check: Subthemes belong to the chosen theme(s) per the taxonomy?
rubric_results.note: 'Industrial Manufacturing and Pollution' is assigned under 'Industrial Production and Materials' which is reasonable, but 'Resource Extraction and Material Flows' under the same theme for rock spoil disposal is a stretch. Cannot fully verify without taxonomy.

rubric_results.check: 1–3 themes and 2–5 subthemes selected (within range)?
rubric_results.note: 3 themes and 4 subthemes — both within the allowed ranges.

themes.notes: No cited page text was provided, making it impossible to verify that the chosen themes and subthemes are grounded in the actual document content. Additionally, the fixed taxonomy cannot be checked without its reference list, raising uncertainty about whether theme and subtheme names are verbatim matches. A human reviewer should confirm taxonomy compliance and source-text support before accepting this extraction.

rubric_results.check: Place names appear in cited pages?
rubric_results.note: Chicago, Cook County, Lower Des Plaines Tunnel System, and Illinois all appear extensively throughout the cited page range (1-30, which maps to the executive summary and early EIS chapters).

rubric_results.check: is_multi_site set correctly?
rubric_results.note: The EIS explicitly covers three tunnel systems (Mainstream, Calumet, Lower Des Plaines) plus reservoir sites across Cook County; is_multi_site should be true, not false.

rubric_results.check: For corridor projects: endpoints named, not just a midpoint?
rubric_results.note: The Lower Des Plaines Tunnel System is a corridor (~26 miles) but no specific endpoints (e.g., northern terminus to southern terminus) are named in the extracted value; only the general Chicago/Cook County region is cited.

rubric_results.check: State abbreviations match U.S. conventions?
rubric_results.note: IL is the correct USPS abbreviation for Illinois and is used consistently.

location.notes: The extraction correctly identifies the Lower Des Plaines Tunnel System in Cook County, IL, and uses proper state abbreviations. However, is_multi_site is incorrectly set to false—the EIS covers three tunnel systems (Mainstream, Calumet, Lower Des Plaines) plus multiple reservoir sites, making it a multi-site document. Additionally, the corridor endpoints for the ~26-mile Lower Des Plaines tunnel are not captured. The extraction should be revised to set is_multi_site to true and, where possible, identify the terminal points of the tunnel corridor.

rubric_results.check: Each agency preparer named in the cited Preparers/Consultation text?
rubric_results.note: The cited text explicitly states 'Prepared By The UNITED STATES ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY REGION V' and 'BOOZ, ALLEN AND HAMILTON, INC.' and 'APPROVED BY: GEORGE R. ALEXANDER, JR. REGIONAL ADMINISTRATOR' — all three match the extracted agency_preparers.

rubric_results.check: Cooperating agencies / tribal nations actually listed in the cited text?
rubric_results.note: All extracted cooperating agencies appear in the 'Federal, State, and Local Agencies and Individuals Notified' section of the cited text. Minor OCR variants (e.g., 'Fish and Wildlife Servies') do not affect identification. No tribal nations are listed in the source text, and none were extracted.

rubric_results.check: For public_commenters: stance present on cited page? Quote verbatim? Speaker clear?
rubric_results.note: No public commenters were extracted; the extracted value correctly notes public_commenters as an empty list and comment_response_present as false.

rubric_results.check: Private individuals: last-name-only or 'private commenter' convention followed?
rubric_results.note: No private individuals were extracted as commenters. Congressional representatives are listed in the notification section but were not extracted as key people, which is appropriate.

key_people.notes: All three agency preparers (EPA Region V, Booz Allen & Hamilton, and George R. Alexander Jr.) are clearly present in the cited text and correctly extracted. All cooperating/notified agencies in the extracted list are verifiable in the cited notification section. No public commenters or private individuals were extracted, consistent with the source text being a draft EIS cover/summary with no comment-response section present.
